content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
August 10, 1994

Carl Borden
Associate Counsel
California Farm Bureau Federation
1601 Exposition Boulevard, FB3
Sacramento, CA 95815-5318

Re: Your Request for Confirmation
Of Telephone Advice
Our File No. A-94-251

Dear Mr. Borden:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided
to you concerning the campaign disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act.1

QUESTION

Is a payment made by California Farm Bureau Federation (CFBF)
for hayride services provided at a public picnic organized by
Assembly Speaker Willie Brown an in-kind contribution to one of
his campaign committees?

CONCLUSION

The payment made by CFBF is an in-kind contribution to the
Willie Brown for Assembly 1994 Committee (WBAC).

FACTS

A staffer at the Assembly Office of Research inquired if CFBF
would be interested in paying for some of the costs to be incurred
at a public picnic being organized by Speaker Brown. CFBF agreed
to pay a hayride operator to provide hayrides at the picnic. CFBF
paid $200 directly to the operator by check.

The picnic is an annual event and is not a fundraiser. This
event is open to the public and each attendee is asked but not
required to donate canned food to be distributed to the needy.

_______________________
1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Borden, Carl
File No. A-94-251
Page 2


     CBFB received from WBAC a form titled "Re: Non-Monetary (In-
Kind) Contribution" as a result of the payment made for the
hayride.

     You have indicated that CFBF neither regarded nor intended
its payment for the hayride to be a political contribution and it
was not made for political purposes.

                              ANALYSIS

     Regulation 18215 provides that:

     (a)  A contribution is any monetary or nonmonetary
payment for political purposes for which full and
adequate consideration is not made for the donor.  A
payment is made for political purposes if it is:

          (1)  For the purpose of influencing or attempting to
influence the action of the voters for or against the
nomination or election of a candidate or candidates, or
the qualification or passage of any measure; or

          (2)  Received or made at the behest of:

               (A)  A candidate, unless it is clear from surrounding
circumstances that the payment was received or made at
his or her behest for personal purposes unrelated to his
or her candidacy or status as an office holder.  The
term "payment" includes the candidate's own money or
property used on behalf of his or her candidacy.

               (B)  A controlled committee;...

     (b)  "Made at the behest" means a payment made under the
control or at the direction of, in cooperation,
consultation, coordination, or concert with, or at the
request or suggestion of a candidate, controlled
committee, official committee of a political party, or
organization formed or existing primarily for political
purposes.

     From the facts given it appears that the payment to the
hayride operator was made at the behest of Speaker Brown and
therefore falls within the definition of contribution.

Borden, Carl
File No. A-94-251
Page 3


If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5662.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel
[signature]
By: Wayne P. Imberi
Political Reform Consultant

Borden,
Carl

QUESTION: Is a payment made by California Farm Bureau Federation
(CFBF)
for hayride services provided at a public picnic organized by
Assembly Speaker Willie Brown an in-kind contribution to one of
his campaign committees?

CONCLUSION: The payment made by CFBF is an in-kind contribution to the
Willie Brown for Assembly 1994 Committee
(WBAC).
